summary: Risk archetype r_kelly_safe_size_filter is mathematically sound — both novel_dim conditions parse cleanly (liquidity_bucket categorical + taker_buy_ratio_5m numeric), structurally distinct from existing 40 risk archetypes (no prior combines liquidity AND taker_buy as conjunctive size-gate), with adequate-to-ample expected sample size and broad exit compatibility favoring the proven fixed-exit family.

distinct_from_existing_ids: R001-R040 (no existing risk archetype documented as combining liquidity_bucket=high AND taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60 as conjunctive size-gating filter)
analyses.distinctness_notes: Distinct on two axes: (1) conjunctive AND of a categorical liquidity gate with a continuous taker-flow threshold for sizing rather than entry filtering; (2) framed as kelly-safe size enforcement, which targets the R2-identified E126 asymmetric TP/SL trap by demanding flow confirmation before allocating capital. Even if a similar single-condition risk archetype exists, the AND-conjunction creates a materially different conditional distribution.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Channel=* aggregates ~7200 events/30d (1400 OI + 5000 pc + 800 R6). liquidity_bucket=high cut ~ 0.30 of universe (high-liq symbols dominate volume but not event count) → ~2160. taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60 is a moderately strong long-flow filter cutting ~0.25 of remaining (taker buy ratios skew around 0.50 with std ~0.08, so ≥0.60 ≈ top quartile) → 2160 × 0.25 ≈ 540. side=both keeps full count.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Both conditions are stationary at T0, so this risk gate composes with any of the 5 exit kernels. Strongest fit with fixed exits (the only exit family with positive mean per coverage_summary, mean=+0.175, n_positive=6680/7320) — kelly-safe sizing is most meaningful when TP/SL are symmetric and pre-committed. Secondary fit: breakeven (also bounded left tail). Weak fit: time_only (no TP/SL, sizing gate adds little). Recommend Synthesizer prioritize fixed-exit pairings to directly counter the E126 asymmetric-trap lesson from R2.
